title: [SLP] Fix logic for commuting copyable operands in buildInstructionsState()
author: bababuck
description:
Noticed by inspection, if my understanding is correct the existing logic is incorrect.

for the following:
%add = add i32 %0, %1
%add = add i32 3, %3
%add = add i32 %4, %5
%urem = urem i32 %6, %7

Operands would contain (since `urem`  will be `Copyable`):
```
Operands[0] = {%0, 3, %4, %6}
Operands[1] = {%1, %3, %5, %7}
```
With the existing logic, we would first compare `%0` and `%6`, and then compare `%1` and `%7`. What I believe is intended is to compare `%0` and `%1`, then `3` and `%3`, and so on.

tests.test_body:
define void @test() {
bb:
  br label %bb1

bb1:                                              ; preds = %bb1, %bb
  %phi = phi i32 [ 0, %bb ], [ %or21, %bb1 ]
  %sitofp = sitofp i32 0 to float
  %fadd = fadd float %sitofp, %sitofp
  %fadd2 = fadd float %fadd, 0.000000e+00
  %fcmp = fcmp ogt float %fadd2, 0.000000e+00
  %select = select i1 %fcmp, i32 0, i32 0
  %select3 = select i1 false, i32 %select, i32 0
  %fadd4 = fadd float %sitofp, 0.000000e+00
  %fadd5 = fadd float %fadd4, 0.000000e+00
  %fcmp6 = fcmp ogt float %fadd5, 0.000000e+00
  %select7 = select i1 %fcmp6, i32 0, i32 0
  %select8 = select i1 false, i32 %select7, i32 0
  %or = or i32 %select3, %select8
  %sitofp9 = sitofp i32 0 to float
  %fmul = fmul float 0.000000e+00, 0.000000e+00
  %fadd10 = fadd float %sitofp9, %fmul
  %fadd11 = fadd float %fadd10, 0.000000e+00
  %fcmp12 = fcmp ogt float %fadd11, 0.000000e+00
  %select13 = select i1 %fcmp12, i32 0, i32 0
  %select14 = select i1 false, i32 %select13, i32 0
  %or15 = or i32 %select14, %or
  %fadd16 = fadd float %fmul, 0.000000e+00
  %fadd17 = fadd float %fadd16, 0.000000e+00
  %fcmp18 = fcmp ogt float %fadd17, 0.000000e+00
  %select19 = select i1 %fcmp18, i32 0, i32 0
  %select20 = select i1 false, i32 %select19, i32 0
  %or21 = or i32 %or15, %select20
  br label %bb1
}

define void @test(i32 %arg) {
bb:
  br label %bb1

bb1:                                              ; preds = %bb
  %sub = sub i32 0, %arg
  %add = add i32 7, 0
  %icmp = icmp ult i32 %add, 0
  br i1 false, label %bb2, label %bb3

bb2:                                              ; preds = %bb1
  br label %bb3

bb3:                                              ; preds = %bb2, %bb1
  %phi = phi i32 [ 7, %bb2 ], [ 0, %bb1 ]
  %phi4 = phi i32 [ %sub, %bb2 ], [ %sub, %bb1 ]
  %phi5 = phi i32 [ %add, %bb2 ], [ %add, %bb1 ]
  %phi6 = phi i32 [ %add, %bb2 ], [ 0, %bb1 ]
  ret void
}

define float @test() {
bb:
  %fadd = fadd float 0.000000e+00, 0.000000e+00
  %load = load float, ptr null, align 4
  %fadd1 = fadd float %fadd, %load
  %fadd2 = fadd float 0.000000e+00, 0.000000e+00
  %fmul = fmul float 0.000000e+00, 0.000000e+00
  %fmul3 = fmul float 0.000000e+00, 0.000000e+00
  %fadd4 = fadd float %fmul3, 0.000000e+00
  br i1 false, label %bb5, label %bb6

bb5:                                              ; preds = %bb
  br label %bb8

bb6:                                              ; preds = %bb
  %fdiv = fdiv float %fadd1, 0.000000e+00
  %fdiv7 = fdiv float %fadd2, 0.000000e+00
  br label %bb8

bb8:                                              ; preds = %bb6, %bb5
  %phi = phi float [ 0.000000e+00, %bb5 ], [ %fdiv7, %bb6 ]
  %phi9 = phi float [ %fmul, %bb5 ], [ 0.000000e+00, %bb6 ]
  %phi10 = phi float [ %fadd4, %bb5 ], [ %load, %bb6 ]
  %phi11 = phi float [ %fadd1, %bb5 ], [ %fdiv, %bb6 ]
  ret float %phi11
}

define i32 @test(i32 %add.i64326, i32 %call119) {
entry:
  %add.r = add i32 0, 0
  %sext.mask = and i32 %add.r, 0
  %call = tail call i32 (...) null()
  %sext.mask323 = and i32 %call, 0
  %call35 = tail call i32 (...) null()
  %sext.mask324 = and i32 %call35, 0
  %add.r63 = add i32 0, 0
  %sext.mask325 = and i32 %add.r63, 0
  %coerce36.sroa.2.0.extract.shift327 = add i32 %add.i64326, %call35
  %call91 = tail call i32 (...) null()
  %sext329.mask = and i32 %call91, 0
  %0 = and i32 %call91, 0
  %add.r131 = add i32 %call119, %add.i64326
  %sext332.mask = and i32 %add.r131, 0
  %coerce52.sroa.2.0.extract.shift335 = add i32 0, 0
  %call1193 = tail call i32 (...) null()
  %sext336.mask = and i32 %call1193, 0
  %add.i132334 = and i32 %call1193, 0
  %call211 = tail call i32 (...) null()
  %sext339.mask = and i32 %call211, 0
  %1 = and i32 %call211, 0
  %call239 = tail call i32 (...) null()
  %sext342.mask = and i32 %call239, 0
  %add.i320 = and i32 %call, 0
  %tobool31.not = icmp eq i32 %add.i320, 0
  %add33 = select i1 %tobool31.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %tobool25.not = icmp eq i32 %sext.mask323, 0
  %add27 = select i1 %tobool25.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add12 = add i32 %add33, %add27
  %tobool.not = icmp ult i32 0, 0
  %add11 = select i1 %tobool.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add28 = add i32 %add12, %add11
  %tobool47.not = icmp ult i32 1, 0
  %add49 = select i1 %tobool47.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add34 = add i32 %add28, %add49
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %sext.mask, 0
  %add = select i1 %cmp, i32 0, i32 0
  %add44 = add i32 %add34, %add
  %tobool41.not = icmp eq i32 %sext.mask324, 0
  %add43 = select i1 %tobool41.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add50 = add i32 %add44, %add43
  %tobool87.not = icmp ult i32 0, 0
  %add89 = select i1 %tobool87.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add72 = add i32 %add50, %add89
  %add159 = select i1 %tobool87.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add78 = add i32 %add72, %add159
  %add83 = select i1 poison, i32 0, i32 0
  %add84 = add i32 %add78, %add83
  %add152 = select i1 poison, i32 0, i32 0
  %add90 = add i32 %add84, %add152
  %tobool69.not = icmp eq i32 %sext.mask325, 0
  %add71 = select i1 %tobool69.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add112 = add i32 %add90, %add71
  %tobool75.not = icmp ult i32 %coerce36.sroa.2.0.extract.shift327, 0
  %add77 = select i1 %tobool75.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add118 = add i32 %add112, %add77
  %tobool109.not = icmp eq i32 %sext329.mask, 0
  %add111 = select i1 %tobool109.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add140 = add i32 %add118, %add111
  %tobool115.not = icmp eq i32 %0, 0
  %add117 = select i1 %tobool115.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add146 = add i32 %add140, %add117
  %tobool175.not = icmp eq i32 %sext336.mask, 0
  %add177 = select i1 %tobool175.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add153 = add i32 %add146, %add177
  %tobool181.not = icmp eq i32 %add.i132334, 0
  %add183 = select i1 %tobool181.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add160 = add i32 %add153, %add183
  %tobool137.not = icmp eq i32 %sext332.mask, 0
  %add139 = select i1 %tobool137.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add178 = add i32 %add160, %add139
  %tobool143.not = icmp ult i32 %coerce52.sroa.2.0.extract.shift335, 0
  %add145 = select i1 %tobool143.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add184 = add i32 %add178, %add145
  %add202 = select i1 %tobool137.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add203 = add i32 %add184, %add202
  %add209 = select i1 %tobool143.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add210 = add i32 %add203, %add209
  %tobool229.not = icmp eq i32 %sext339.mask, 0
  %add231 = select i1 %tobool229.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add232 = add i32 %add210, %add231
  %tobool235.not = icmp eq i32 %1, 0
  %add237 = select i1 %tobool235.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add238 = add i32 %add232, %add237
  %tobool251.not = icmp ult i32 0, 0
  %add253 = select i1 %tobool251.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add248 = add i32 %add238, %add253
  %tobool245.not = icmp eq i32 %sext342.mask, 0
  %add247 = select i1 %tobool245.not, i32 0, i32 0
  %add254 = add i32 %add248, %add247
  ret i32 %add254
}

define i1 @test(float %0, double %1) {
  %3 = fpext float %0 to double
  %4 = fpext float 0.000000e+00 to double
  %5 = fpext float 0.000000e+00 to double
  %6 = fpext float 0.000000e+00 to double
  %7 = fmul double 0.000000e+00, 0.000000e+00
  %8 = fmul double 0.000000e+00, %1
  %9 = fmul double 0.000000e+00, 0.000000e+00
  %10 = fmul double 0.000000e+00, %5
  %11 = fmul double 0.000000e+00, %6
  %12 = fsub double %10, %11
  %13 = fptrunc double %12 to float
  %14 = fmul double %9, 0.000000e+00
  %15 = fmul double 0.000000e+00, %3
  %16 = fsub double %14, %15
  %17 = fptrunc double %16 to float
  %18 = fptrunc double %7 to float
  %19 = fmul double %1, %6
  %20 = fmul double 0.000000e+00, %4
  %21 = fsub double %19, %20
  %22 = fptrunc double %21 to float
  %23 = fsub double 0.000000e+00, %8
  %24 = fptrunc double %23 to float
  %25 = fmul double 0.000000e+00, 0.000000e+00
  %26 = fptrunc double %25 to float
  %27 = fmul double %9, %4
  %28 = fmul double 0.000000e+00, %5
  %29 = fsub double %27, %28
  %30 = fptrunc double %29 to float
  %31 = fmul double %9, 0.000000e+00
  %32 = fptrunc double %31 to float
  %33 = fmul float %13, 0.000000e+00
  %34 = fcmp oeq float %33, 0.000000e+00
  %35 = fmul float %22, 0.000000e+00
  %36 = fcmp oeq float %35, 0.000000e+00
  %37 = select i1 %34, i1 %36, i1 false
  %38 = fmul float %30, 0.000000e+00
  %39 = fcmp oeq float %38, 0.000000e+00
  %40 = select i1 %37, i1 %39, i1 false
  %41 = fmul float %17, 0.000000e+00
  %42 = fcmp oeq float %41, 0.000000e+00
  %43 = select i1 %40, i1 %42, i1 false
  %44 = fmul float %24, 0.000000e+00
  %45 = fcmp oeq float %44, 0.000000e+00
  %46 = select i1 %43, i1 %45, i1 false
  %47 = fmul float %32, 0.000000e+00
  %48 = fcmp oeq float %47, 0.000000e+00
  %49 = select i1 %46, i1 %48, i1 false
  %50 = fmul float %18, 0.000000e+00
  %51 = fcmp oeq float %50, 0.000000e+00
  %52 = select i1 %49, i1 %51, i1 false
  %53 = fmul float %26, 0.000000e+00
  %54 = fcmp oeq float %53, 0.000000e+00
  %55 = select i1 %52, i1 %54, i1 false
  ret i1 %55
}

comments.author: github-actions[bot]
comments.body:
<!--LLVM CODE FORMAT COMMENT: {undef deprecator}-->


:warning: undef deprecator found issues in your code. :warning:

<details>
<summary>
You can test this locally with the following command:
</summary>

``````````bash
git diff -U0 --pickaxe-regex -S '([^a-zA-Z0-9#_-]undef([^a-zA-Z0-9_-]|$)|UndefValue::get)' 'HEAD~1' HEAD llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/SLPVectorizer.cpp llvm/test/Transforms/SLPVectorizer/X86/bv-root-part-of-graph.ll llvm/test/Transforms/SLPVectorizer/X86/copyable-with-used-outside-bb.ll llvm/test/Transforms/SLPVectorizer/X86/non-schedulable-node-with-non-schedulable-parent.ll llvm/test/Transforms/SLPVectorizer/X86/split-vectorize-gathered-def-after-use.ll llvm/test/Transforms/SLPVectorizer/extract-many-users-buildvector.ll
``````````

</details>


The following files introduce new uses of undef:
 - llvm/test/Transforms/SLPVectorizer/X86/bv-root-part-of-graph.ll
 - llvm/test/Transforms/SLPVectorizer/X86/non-schedulable-node-with-non-schedulable-parent.ll

[Undef](https://llvm.org/docs/LangRef.html#undefined-values) is now deprecated and should only be used in the rare cases where no replacement is possible. For example, a load of uninitialized memory yields `undef`. You should use `poison` values for placeholders instead.

In tests, avoid using `undef` and having tests that trigger undefined behavior. If you need an operand with some unimportant value, you can add a new argument to the function and use that instead.

For example, this is considered a bad practice:
```llvm
define void @fn() {
  ...
  br i1 undef, ...
}
```

Please use the following instead:
```llvm
define void @fn(i1 %cond) {
  ...
  br i1 %cond, ...
}
```

Please refer to the [Undefined Behavior Manual](https://llvm.org/docs/UndefinedBehavior.html) for more information.

comments.author: alexey-bataev
comments.body:
> Noticed by inspection, if my understanding is correct the existing logic is incorrect.
> 
> for the following: %add = add i32 %0, %1 %add = add i32 3, %3 %add = add i32 %4, %5 %urem = urem i32 %6, %7
> 
> Operands would contain (since `urem` will be `Copyable`):
> 
> ```
> Operands[0] = {%0, 3, %4, %6}
> Operands[1] = {%1, %3, %5, %7}
> ```
> 
> With the existing logic, we would first compare `%0` and `%6`, and then compare `%1` and `%7`. What I believe is intended is to compare `%0` and `%1`, then `3` and `%3`, and so on.

No, current logic is correct. We need to find matching operands by lanes, not by operands, so comparing 0 nd 6 and 1 and 7 is what is supposed to be

comments.author: bababuck
comments.body:
> > Noticed by inspection, if my understanding is correct the existing logic is incorrect.
> > for the following: %add = add i32 %0, %1 %add = add i32 3, %3 %add = add i32 %4, %5 %urem = urem i32 %6, %7
> > Operands would contain (since `urem` will be `Copyable`):
> > ```
> > Operands[0] = {%0, 3, %4, %6}
> > Operands[1] = {%1, %3, %5, %7}
> > ```
> > 
> > With the existing logic, we would first compare `%0` and `%6`, and then compare `%1` and `%7`. What I believe is intended is to compare `%0` and `%1`, then `3` and `%3`, and so on.
> 
> No, current logic is correct. We need to find matching operands by lanes, not by operands, so comparing 0 nd 6 and 1 and 7 is what is supposed to be.

Let's assume %0 is not an instruction, so when we call `std::swap(Ops.front(), Ops.back())`, in the original implementation, we would be updating.
```
Operands[0] = {%0, 3, %4, %6}
Operands[1] = {%1, %3, %5, %7}
```
to
```
Operands[0] = {%6, 3, %4, %0}
Operands[1] = {%1, %3, %5, %7}
```
this seems wrong to me because effectively we swapped the operands between instructions:
before:
```
%add = add i32 %0, %1
...
%add3 = add i32 %6, %7
```
After:
```
%add = add i32 %6, %1
...
%add3 = add i32 %0, %7
```
